obligations.reason: Debug bus master paths (AXI/LINT) may bypass normal access controls to protected memory/peripherals if not properly gated by debug authorization.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug interface (adbg_axi_module, adbg_lint_module)
obligations.object: protected SoC memory/peripherals (e.g., APB peripherals, TCDM, ROM)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug authorization signal (e.g., dbg_auth, lock bit) or lifecycle state preventing debug transactions until explicitly enabled
obligations.candidate_path: Debug module (AXI or LINT BIU) -> SoC bus (AXI master / LINT bus) -> target memory/peripheral
state_conditions_to_check: Authorization signal from a trusted source
required_evidence_slots: Signal showing debug request path from module to bus
Authorization check logic (e.g., gating of bus valid)
Address range check for protected regions
obligations.uncertainty: Exact authorization signal name and location to be identified in RTL.

obligations.reason: Lock bits in debug modules must prevent any further configuration changes after being set; failure may allow reconfiguration and privilege escalation.
obligations.subject: software/debug command
obligations.operation: write to lock bit configuration register
obligations.object: debug module configuration registers (control registers, access enables)
obligations.expected_guard: Once lock bit is set, it must gate all write enables to protected registers and cannot be cleared without a reset or higher authority.
obligations.candidate_path: Debug register access via JTAG TAP's DR chain (update_dr) -> register write logic
state_conditions_to_check: Lock bit set/clear conditions (reset, write, other events)
Write enable gating logic for configuration registers
required_evidence_slots: Lock bit flop and its set/clear logic
Write enable equations for protected registers
obligations.uncertainty: Need to locate specific lock register within debug modules.

obligations.reason: Internal secret state (e.g., CRC computation, debug data) must not be exposed via TDO without proper authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG user via TDO
obligations.operation: capture and shift of debug data registers
obligations.object: internal CRC state, debug module data registers, or other sensitive state
obligations.expected_guard: Sensitive data output from TDO is gated by debug authorization; unauthorized reads return zero or constant value, or sensitive state is cleared before shift.
obligations.candidate_path: Internal registers (e.g., adbg_crc32 output) -> data_register_i -> TDO shift logic
state_conditions_to_check: Module select asserted for sensitive module
capture_dr_i / shift_dr_i state in TAP FSM
required_evidence_slots: CRC or other sensitive register output connectivity
obligations.uncertainty: Must determine if CRC32 is used for security or merely debug; still a potential leak.

obligations.reason: Reset default of debug control/lock flops may leave debug interface enabled and accessible without explicit enablement, violating secure boot assumptions.
obligations.subject: system reset
obligations.object: debug module configuration registers (lock, enable, access control bits)
obligations.expected_guard: After reset, debug bus access is disabled (e.g., lock bit set, enable bit cleared) until a trusted software sequence enables it.
obligations.candidate_path: Reset value of flops -> subsequent unconditional enable of bus access
state_conditions_to_check: Enable bits for debug bus requests
required_evidence_slots: Default values in RTL or define files
obligations.uncertainty: Need to verify if debug is expected to be locked at reset.

obligations.reason: OR1K debug module may bypass processor privilege checks when accessing internal CPU status registers, exposing sensitive execution state.
obligations.subject: debug interface via OR1K module
obligations.object: OR1K processor internal state (status register, control registers)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug access to processor state is only allowed in debug mode and/or when debug authorization is asserted; not accessible in normal operation.
required_evidence_slots: Any privilege check before granting access
obligations.uncertainty: Assumes OR1K debug can change processor state; need to verify actual security impact.

obligations.reason: TAP controller module_select signals must be gated with debug authorization; otherwise an external JTAG user can activate debug modules without authentication.
obligations.subject: JTAG TAP controller (adbg_tap_top)
obligations.operation: select debug module via IR/DR state
obligations.expected_guard: module_select outputs are only asserted when a corresponding debug authorization flag is set (e.g., from lock register or external auth controller).
obligations.candidate_path: TAP FSM -> module_select generation -> adbg_axi_module / adbg_lint_module / adbg_or1k_module
required_evidence_slots: Logic generating module_select_i for debug modules
Auth signal presence in that logic
obligations.uncertainty: May use module_select directly; need to check if any gating exists.

obligations.reason: Bus fabric may not enforce security attributes (e.g., AxPROT, secure bit) between debug-initiated transactions and protected regions, allowing debug masters to access secure memory.
obligations.subject: debug bus master (AXI or LINT)
obligations.object: secure memory regions (e.g., ROM, L2 memory, safe domain)
obligations.expected_guard: Address decoder checks security attribute or a separate secure access control list to block debug master from secure regions, or debug master's AxPROT is forced to non-secure and rejected by slave.
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_axi_biu -> AXI bus -> SoC address decoder -> secure memory controller
state_conditions_to_check: AxPROT or equivalent security signal driven by debug module
Address decode rule for secure regions
obligations.uncertainty: Might not be a requirement if debug is trusted; still worth checking.

obligations.reason: Data buffers (bytefifo) used in debug BIU may retain data from previous transactions, leaking sensitive information to a subsequent debug session.
obligations.subject: internal debug buffer (bytefifo)
obligations.object: buffered data from previous debug transaction
obligations.expected_guard: Buffer is cleared on reset, on debug session termination, or when lock/unlock events occur; no stale data accessible.
obligations.candidate_path: bytefifo read port -> debug output (lint_rdata or similar)
state_conditions_to_check: Buffer clear signal connected to reset or lock events
required_evidence_slots: Connection of clear signal to debug control
obligations.uncertainty: Accuracy depends on actual use of bytefifo in debug data path.
